source.title: The Option-Critic Architecture
source.venue: Proceedings of the AAAI Conference on Artificial Intelligence (AAAI-17)

confidence_rationale: The option-critic architecture closes the gap between Sutton-Precup-Singh 1999 (formalism) and ARC-064 (we need an option-discovery algorithm). It derives policy-gradient theorems for both the intra-option policy and the termination condition, and shows that the agent can simultaneously learn options end-to-end from environment reward without subgoals or pseudo-rewards. This is directly the substrate algorithm that ARC-064 + MECH-317 need. Confidence not 1.0 because (a) it needs reward signal so it does not handle the reward-agnostic chunking case, (b) the empirical demonstrations are on Atari/four-rooms, not biological agents, and (c) the discovered options often collapse to short-horizon (the so-called option-collapse problem) which is exactly the kind of failure mode REE's monomodal-collapse motif touches.
failure_signatures: If REE's MECH-317 cannot be implemented as a learned termination condition + intra-option policy + gating layer, the option-critic transfer fails and we need a different inheritance path.

Option-collapse (Bacon 2017's known failure mode: discovered options become trivially short or trivially identical) is the exact REE-side failure pattern observed in V3-EXQ-543 (ARM_1c seed-2 byte-identical to baseline). The diagnostic signatures match across paradigms; the inherited diagnostic tool here is the option-collapse literature.

mapping.source_claim_statement: The option-critic architecture learns the internal policies and termination conditions of options, in tandem with the policy over options, end-to-end from a primary reward signal. Policy gradient theorems are derived for both the intra-option policy and the termination function. The agent does not need pseudo-rewards or pre-specified subgoals.
mapping.ree_translation: Option-critic is what ARC-064's bottom-up rule discovery / MECH-317 behavioural-pattern-compression looks like as a learning algorithm. The option policy = the rule's behavioural content; the termination function = MECH-317's chunk boundary; the policy-over-options = ARC-062's gated policy + discriminator. The whole REE rule-apprehension cluster, viewed through option-critic eyes, is one well-known architecture that has 8+ years of follow-up work (option-collapse mitigations, hierarchical option-critic, regularised termination, etc).
mapping.mapping_caveat: Option-critic requires a reward signal to drive option discovery. REE's ARC-064 includes a reward-agnostic regularity-extraction arm (MECH-316 hippocampal-monosynaptic-CLS-analog) which option-critic does not directly cover -- that is closer to Eysenbach 2018 DIAYN territory. Also: option-critic is empirically demonstrated on Atari and grid-worlds; its transfer to continuous-state biologically-realistic substrates is plausible but not zero-shot. Option-collapse is a well-documented failure mode and is the same diagnostic pattern V3-EXQ-543 hit -- this is a *useful* inherited failure mode, not just a caveat.
mapping.source_context: Computer-science conference paper (AAAI 2017). Empirical demonstrations on Four-Rooms, Pinball, Atari games (Asterix, Ms.Pacman, Seaquest, Zaxxon).

confidence_components.notes: Top-tier ML conference, well-cited, code published. Mapping fidelity high because option-critic is the dominant framework for end-to-end option discovery with reward. Transfer risk moderate-high because the algorithm assumes reward-driven gradients and Atari-style action spaces; biological-substrate translation needs additional work.
